cover.title: 3 ways to feed an agent your repo
cover.sub: Over MCP you pick one: dump everything, retrieve candidate chunks, or serve the one canonical answer. Same goal, very different token bill.

slides.title: 1 · dump the repo
slides.body: Hand the agent the whole slab on every call. Fine for a tiny repo or a one-off. As a default you pay for everything to use a fraction, and a fat window can bury the part that mattered.

slides.title: 2 · retrieve top-k
slides.body: Embed the query, return the closest chunks to rank. Right for broad recall across mixed sources. But similarity has no notion of current, so a stale chunk ranks right next to a fresh one.

slides.title: 3 · serve one answer
slides.body: Return the single current doc: path:line + a freshness marker, section-level, no pile. The agent does zero ranking. the default for doc-heavy, multi-agent repos.

cta.title: pick by your repo, not the hype
cta.sub: Tiny repo → dump. Broad mixed corpus → retrieve. Doc-heavy fleet that needs the current answer cheaply → one canonical answer. Open source: install + star.
